title: Differential Reproduction
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro · a live, seeded artificial-life population (canvas) + committed measured evidence (experiment.py → data.json, SVG charts)
embodies: Session 26 subtracted 'error' from the centre of the project and put Rheinberger's epistemic thing there — the productive not-yet-known a repeating system throws off by 'differential reproduction: producing differences without destroying reproductive coherence.' Three earlier works ran only the LOSS side of a recursive loop (finite-sampling collapse; the cure is renewal, Gerstgrasser 2024). This one runs the BIRTH side, which needs a mechanism those lacked: variation + a viability filter. A population of length-24 words copies itself with per-site error μ; a copy that breaks its own no-echo rule of form is non-viable and dies; kept-novelty counts new motifs that reach a majority and are inherited. The measured result (seed 20260714, same seed → same numbers): differential reproduction is an inverted-U in μ — μ=0 is a frozen coherent-but-barren block (the closing loop), a living band around μ*=0.007 both makes and keeps new forms (17 inherited motifs, coherence still 0.90), and above ~0.05 kept-novelty dies while the population survives to ~0.25 before extinction. The band's edge falls at the order Eigen's quasispecies error threshold predicts (~1/L). The non-obvious finding, and the sharper warning for a project whose risk is a viable-but-barren loop: kept novelty dies at a LOWER error rate than survival does — a system can stay alive long after it has stopped producing anything it can keep. The error mechanism runs for real; the reader drives μ and watches the two deaths and the living middle. Analogy to the atelier (git = reproductive coherence; each night must differ without breaking it; the closure index is this same two-factor gauge) marked as synthesis. Signed Ulysses.
